modules.title: Python core — the Java→Python delta
modules.build: Port a small Java utility you know to idiomatic Python; then 10 micro-scripts, one per feature below, run with `pytest`.

topics.label: data types & mutability (list, tuple, set, dict, str, bytes)

unlocks.text: list vs tuple vs set vs dict (mutability/hashability)

modules.drill: The 5 things that bite Java devs in Python — indentation-as-syntax, dynamic typing, mutable defaults, the GIL, no compile step.

modules.title: Typing, OOP & async
modules.build: A typed module (classes + dunders + `@property` + Pydantic + `mypy` clean); an async script firing 20 concurrent `httpx` calls via `asyncio.gather`, timed vs a sync loop.

topics.label: asyncio, event loop, coroutines, async/await, gather, tasks, async with/async for
threading vs multiprocessing vs asyncio (CPU- vs I/O-bound)

unlocks.text: the GIL and why async (not threads) wins for I/O-bound LLM work but multiprocessing for CPU-bound

modules.drill: Why an LLM gateway is I/O-bound, and how one event loop juggles hundreds of in-flight calls on one thread despite the GIL.

modules.title: The AI service scaffold
modules.build: A typed FastAPI service proxying chat completions — async, Pydantic-validated, streaming, `/health`, validated JSON output, tests. The scaffold for every later project.

topics.label: FastAPI (routing, path/query/body, dependency injection, middleware, background tasks)

unlocks.text: enforce structured output + handle malformed JSON

modules.drill: Stand up a streaming, validated LLM endpoint; explain each layer (router → Pydantic → async client → stream).

modules.title: LLM foundations playground
modules.build: A "model lab": tokenizer visualizer + token counter; temperature/top-p/top-k sliders showing output change; one interface over 3 providers (Anthropic, OpenAI, local Ollama); per-call cost & latency logging.

topics.label: feedforward layers, residual connections, layer normalization
positional encoding (sinusoidal, learned, RoPE, ALiBi) & context-length extrapolation (YaRN)
tokenization (BPE, WordPiece, SentencePiece, vocabulary, special tokens, subwords)
decoding/sampling (greedy, beam, temperature, top-p, top-k, repetition penalty, logits)
training pipeline (pretraining → SFT → RLHF/DPO, reward model, PPO)
scaling laws (Chinchilla 20:1), emergent abilities, inference-time compute / reasoning models
open vs closed models (GPT, Claude, Llama, Mistral, Gemini)

unlocks.text: why positional encoding, RoPE vs ALiBi
BPE vs WordPiece vs SentencePiece + why tokens drive cost/context
context window & what breaks past it
temperature vs top-p vs top-k, logits

modules.drill: Whiteboard attention (Q·Kᵀ → softmax → ·V) in 4 minutes; explain why a 10k-token prompt costs ~10× a 1k one.

modules.title: Production RAG service
modules.build: FastAPI RAG service over a real corpus (few-thousand docs/PDFs): chunk → embed → store (pgvector, then a managed DB) → hybrid retrieval (dense+BM25+RRF) → cross-encoder rerank → generate with citations. Measure NDCG@k before/after rerank. Small UI.

topics.label: RAG pipeline (ingest → chunk → embed → store → retrieve → augment → generate)
document loading & parsing (PDF, tables, layout, OCR, HTML, Markdown)
chunking (fixed-size, overlap, recursive, semantic, parent-child, document-aware) & sizing
vector DBs (pgvector, Qdrant, Weaviate, Pinecone, Chroma, Milvus, FAISS)
ANN indexes (HNSW, IVF, product quantization) & metadata filtering
retrieval (dense, sparse/BM25, hybrid, reciprocal rank fusion, top-k, MMR)
query transformation (HyDE, decomposition, step-back, multi-query, rewriting)
advanced RAG (corrective/CRAG, self-RAG, agentic RAG, GraphRAG, contextual retrieval)
scaling to millions of docs under latency budgets + caching
RAG metrics (faithfulness, answer relevance, context precision/recall, RAGAS)

unlocks.text: hybrid search, RRF, reranking, "lost in the middle"
failure debugging (hallucinating with correct context, slow retrieval, duplicates/dedup, domain jargon, PDF tables)
RAG vs fine-tuning with cost awareness

modules.drill: "Design a RAG system for customer support" end-to-end in 8 min — the most-asked AI system-design question.

modules.title: Agent + MCP
modules.build: A multi-step agent (research/triage over APIs + your RAG store): ReAct loop, tool defs, short/long-term memory, step cap, human approval gate before risky actions, one MCP integration; orchestrate with LangGraph or CrewAI.

topics.label: agent vs chain vs simple call
reasoning loops (ReAct, ReWoo, Plan-and-Execute, Reflexion, LLMCompiler)
tool/function calling, tool design & JSON schemas
memory (in-context buffer, vector/semantic, episodic, procedural, summarization)
single vs multi-agent (orchestrator-worker, supervisor, hierarchical, debate) & why most break past ~5 steps
frameworks (LangChain, LangGraph state machines, LlamaIndex, CrewAI, AutoGen)
MCP (JSON-RPC, transports: stdio/HTTP-SSE/streamable; primitives: tools/resources/prompts/sampling; capability negotiation, federation; security: tool poisoning, TOFU pinning, least-privilege)
human-in-the-loop (approval gates, interruption semantics, durable execution, idempotent resume)
agent failure-mode taxonomy (specification, execution, environmental, alignment)

unlocks.text: single vs multi-agent, orchestrator-worker, why demos break past 5 steps

modules.drill: "Design an insurance-claim (or Spotify-support) agent using RAG with cost control + human fallback" — tools, loop cap, guardrails, escalation.

modules.title: Evaluation + observability harness
modules.build: An eval harness for your RAG + agent: offline set (~150 examples) + online LLM-as-judge over traces + a CI gate that blocks deploys on regression; RAGAS metrics; OpenTelemetry → Langfuse/LangSmith dashboard (latency, tokens, cost-per-task).

topics.label: metrics (task success; faithfulness, answer relevance, context precision/recall; tool-call accuracy; trajectory vs outcome; why BLEU/ROUGE/exact-match are limited)
observability (OpenTelemetry, GenAI semantic conventions, spans, attributes, trace propagation; Langfuse/LangSmith/Helicone)
production metrics (p50/p95/p99 latency, token usage, cost-per-task, error rate)
guardrails (input/output validation, PII, prompt injection, jailbreak, content moderation)

unlocks.text: evaluate a RAG system (faithfulness, relevance, precision/recall)
evaluate an agent (trajectory vs outcome, tool-call accuracy, GAIA)
regression on non-deterministic output + shadow eval
what becomes a span & how trace IDs propagate

modules.drill: "We ship by vibe — make deploys safe": versioned eval set + numeric gate + regression alarm + shadow eval; alert on regression, not absolute thresholds.

modules.title: Fine-tuning run
modules.build: One real QLoRA fine-tune of a small open model (Llama/Mistral 7–8B) on Colab/Lambda for a narrow task; publish the adapter to HF Hub; compare tuned-small vs prompted-large on cost/latency/quality.

topics.label: training types (pretraining, continued pretraining, SFT, instruction tuning, alignment: RLHF/DPO/ORPO)
data prep (instruction formatting, chat templates, loss masking, data quality > quantity)
privacy/security in training data (PII exclusion, masking, provenance)
Hugging Face stack (transformers, peft, trl, datasets, Hub)

unlocks.text: pretraining vs SFT vs instruction tuning vs RLHF vs RAG vs prompt
fintech scenario — fine-tune without exposing sensitive data, exclude/verify training data

modules.drill: RAG-vs-fine-tune decision framework with real numbers (run cost, retraining cadence, forgetting risk).

modules.title: Go live: serving, cost & production system design
modules.build: Deploy one project as a **live, always-on, monitored** service — Dockerized on AWS/GCP, CI/CD, usage+cost tracking, rate limiting, retries, prompt-injection/PII guardrails, public demo link. Keep it running.

topics.label: LLM serving (vLLM, SGLang, TGI; continuous batching; KV-cache reuse; paged attention)
inference optimization (quantization, speculative decoding, prompt caching, semantic caching)
cost optimization (model routing, prompt compression, caching, batching)
scaling (load balancing, autoscaling, rate limiting, queueing, backpressure & overflow path)
latency (streaming, time-to-first-token, SLAs, sync vs async, QPS)
guardrails/safety (prompt injection, PII, content filtering, output validation, jailbreak defense)
security/compliance (access control, audit logging, data residency, RBAC/ABAC)
cloud AI (AWS Bedrock/SageMaker, GCP Vertex, Azure OpenAI)
LLMOps (model & prompt versioning, monitoring, rollback, canary)

unlocks.text: design an LLM-serving system (continuous batching, KV-cache, backpressure + overflow, QPS/SLA, sync vs async)
guardrails (injection, leakage, PII; RBAC/ABAC in pipeline/RAG/prompts)
security/compliance (block unauthorized data, audit/log, prevent output leakage)

modules.drill: "Design an AI system integrating a third party's data + workflows" — ingestion (batch/stream/hybrid) → retrieval/agent → serving (batching/caching) → eval → guardrails → cost controls.

modules.title: Java + Spring
modules.build: Use virtual threads in a concurrent client for your LLM gateway.

topics.label: concurrency (threads, ExecutorService, CompletableFuture, locks, volatile, synchronized, atomics, java.util.concurrent, fork-join, race conditions, deadlocks)
records, sealed classes, pattern matching, switch expressions
Spring (IoC/DI, AOP, bean lifecycle, Boot auto-config, MVC, Data/JPA, @Transactional, Security)

modules.drill: Explain virtual threads vs platform threads; walk a GC pause you'd debug.

modules.title: Databases
modules.build: Tune `pgvector` + metadata queries with `EXPLAIN`; add a Redis cache before RAG retrieval.

topics.label: SQL (joins, aggregations, subqueries, CTEs, window functions)
caching (Redis, cache-aside, write-through, TTL, invalidation, stampede)

modules.drill: Read an EXPLAIN plan; pick isolation level for a given consistency need.

modules.title: Kafka & event-driven
modules.build: Re-architect Project 2's ingestion as a Kafka pipeline (producer → consumer chunks/embeds/indexes).

topics.label: architecture (broker, topic, partition, offset, replication, ISR, leader election)

modules.drill: Achieve exactly-once + ordering in a pipeline; explain the tradeoffs.

modules.title: System design
modules.build: No single build for this module — it's drilled by rehearsing the canon out loud: URL shortener, news feed, chat, rate limiter, distributed cache, payment/ledger, notification service.

topics.label: fundamentals (scalability, availability, reliability, latency vs throughput, consistency)
API design (REST, gRPC, GraphQL, idempotency, pagination)
microservices (gateway, service discovery, circuit breaker, saga, bulkhead)
AI system design (RAG platform, multi-tenant LLM gateway, agent orchestration with cost controls)

modules.drill: Method: clarify → estimate → high-level → deep-dive one component → bottlenecks/tradeoffs. **Your edge:** fuse both tracks in one answer.

modules.title: DSA (background drip)
modules.build: Solve in Python (doubles as language fluency). ~3–5/week, daily in the final 2–3 weeks. Heavy only if the target tests it.

topics.label: graphs (BFS, DFS, Dijkstra, union-find, topological sort)
dynamic programming (1-D, 2-D, knapsack, LIS, edit distance)

modules.drill: Pick one pattern above at random and solve + explain the approach out loud before coding.

modules.title: Behavioral round
modules.weeks: Prep now → ramp in final 3 weeks
modules.build: No artifact to build here — rehearse the stories behind the artifacts you already shipped (RAG service, agent+eval harness, QLoRA run, live deployment) using the STAR method.

topics.label: a confident-then-wrong story (show the debugging path)
a production incident diagnosed via traces

unlocks.text: Tell the story of the end-to-end live LLM system you shipped
Walk through an incident you debugged
Describe a hard tradeoff you made

modules.drill: Tell your "why the switch to AI" story and one production incident, each in under 90 seconds, with a quantified result.

notes.capstone:
Your shipped artifacts are arranged so one project answers many rounds:

- **RAG service** → LLM fundamentals, RAG architecture, vector DBs, system design, behavioral.
- **Agent + eval harness** → agents, MCP, evaluation, observability, failure modes.
- **QLoRA run** → fine-tuning, RAG-vs-finetune, security/compliance.
- **Live deployment** → serving, cost/latency, guardrails, the "I operate production AI" story.

Every answer starts with "When I built X, I hit exactly this — here's what I did and the number it moved." That sentence wins the ₹25–50 LPA band.
notes.finalWeeks:
- **Daily:** 2 system-design or RAG/agent-design drills out loud.
- **Mocks:** 2–3/week (one AI technical, one system design, one core-eng) — peer or LLM as interviewer.
- **Flashcards:** run your own TOPICS + UNLOCKS lists; any gap → reopen the build and break/fix that part.
- **Per company:** map the JD to these modules; rehearse the 3 likeliest questions.
- Keep the live demo running and READMEs sharp — interviewers click them.
